markers.evidence_type: microphthalmia-associated transcription factor, master regulator of melanocyte identity

other_findings.finding: Melanocytes are neural crest-derived cells that migrate to the epidermis and produce melanin pigment stored in melanosomes, which are transferred to keratinocytes to provide UV protection
MITF is the master transcription factor controlling melanocyte identity and is required for expression of melanin synthesis enzymes including TYR, TYRP1, and DCT
Melanocytes in the epidermis form melanin units with surrounding keratinocytes; one melanocyte serves approximately 36 keratinocytes

evidence_quotes.quote: PMEL, MITF, MLANA, TYRP1, DCT are defining markers of melanocytes in single-cell RNA-seq studies of human skin
evidence_quotes.source_file: Shi et al. (2025) Human skin single-cell atlas

evidence_quotes.quote: MITF is essential for melanocyte survival, proliferation, and differentiation, and directly activates transcription of melanin synthesis genes TYR, TYRP1 and DCT
